Our professional ant extermination services target common species found in Washington Heights' pre-war buildings, brownstones, and modern apartments. whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Washington Heights for years, understanding the specific pest pressures of this Manhattan neighborhood from Fort Tryon Park to the Hudson River waterfront. localContext: Washington Heights' mix of historic buildings, tree-lined streets near Highbridge Park, and proximity to green spaces creates ideal conditions for ant infestations, especially during warmer months. The neighborhood's classic architecture, including many walk-ups and buildings near Broadway, often provides multiple entry points that our experienced team knows how to seal and treat effectively.

faqs.question: What types of ants are common in Washington Heights apartments?
faqs.answer: Washington Heights typically sees pavement ants, odorous house ants, and carpenter ants. Pavement ants are most common in ground-floor units and near building foundations, while carpenter ants can be found in older buildings with wooden structures. Our technicians identify the species during inspection to apply the most effective treatment method for your specific ant problem.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide ant control service in Washington Heights?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout Washington Heights, from the streets near Columbia Presbyterian to the Fort Tryon area. Most treatments take 1-2 hours to complete, and you'll see significant reduction in ant activity within 24-48 hours. Our team is familiar with parking and building access throughout the neighborhood for efficient service.

faqs.question: Why does ant control cost $350-$550 in Washington Heights?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the size of your apartment or home, severity of the infestation, and ant species involved. A studio or one-bedroom typically runs $350-$425, while larger apartments or multi-level homes cost $450-$550. The price includes thorough inspection, interior and exterior treatment, crack and crevice sealing, and a follow-up visit if needed within 30 days.

faqs.question: Are your ant treatments safe for families and pets in Washington Heights homes?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for children and pets when applied by our licensed technicians. We can work around your schedule and provide specific re-entry times, typically 2-4 hours after treatment. Many Washington Heights families with young children and pets trust our low-toxicity treatment methods that effectively eliminate ants while prioritizing safety.
